target_semantics: Start in normal state and support a reliable normal-to-insert-to-normal editing roundtrip.
divergence: none
notes: Scope is the tested startup and insert roundtrip, not every Evil command.

target_semantics: Use Space as leader in normal and visual states and resolve every declared Lem leader chord to its intended command.
divergence: none
notes: Command-level parity is tracked separately where implementations diverge.

target_semantics: Send the active region, otherwise buffer text through the end of the current word or punctuation, to the LLM with C-c i.
divergence: Without a region Lem stops exactly at point, while installed gptel advances a mid-token point over remaining word or punctuation syntax; Lem exposes the region path from native VISUAL because selecting enters that state, and successful backends focus a separate output buffer rather than inserting the response at the source position.
notes: The real ncurses test invokes C-c i against an isolated backend and verifies exact-once dispatch, outer-whitespace trimming, blank refusal, end-of-token, mid-word, and mid-punctuation text through point from INSERT, inclusive forward and reverse character regions from VISUAL, source preservation, and dispatch from the expected state; network transport is tracked separately by AI-001.

target_semantics: C-u in insert state deletes back to indentation without leaving insert state.
divergence: none
notes: none

target_semantics: C-n and C-p remain ordinary logical-line movement outside completion-specific keymaps.
divergence: none
notes: Emacs achieves this by unbinding Evil overrides; Lem binds the intended fall-through behavior directly.

target_semantics: Provide the configured global window, duplicate, recent-file, grep, find-name, and buffer-list workflows on their expected bindings.
divergence: Find-name lacks Dired marking, file operations, and process cancellation; list-buffers lacks the configured Ibuffer saved groups; and visual-block or rectangle duplication remains unsupported.
notes: The TUI suite exercises M-j, M-g r, M-s f, and C-x C-b; M-o and M-s g remain source-verified.

target_semantics: Provide mode-local Claude Code, Notmuch payment-email, Elfeed archive, Salta, and project outline bindings.
divergence: No project-local consult-outline binding and Notmuch payment-email bridge is not reproduced.
notes: Individual app behavior remains separately tracked.

target_semantics: Support ys, ds, cs, visual S, and padded delimiter surround editing while retaining native operators.
divergence: Tag prompts and syntax-aware balancing are missing.
notes: Core tested grammar works, but the known missing grammar prevents an exact claim.

target_semantics: Match the configured evil-snipe 2.1.3 motions, scopes, repeat maps, operators, highlighting, cancellation, and whitespace behavior.
divergence: none
notes: The real TUI gate covers case-sensitive s/S/f/F/t/T, initial visible and repeat whole-visible scopes, counts and dot replay, persistent and transient direction reversal, z/Z/x/X endpoints, Return reuse, leading whitespace, EOF and overlap edges, incremental/final faces, cancellation, reload, and jumplist behavior.

target_semantics: Use gc as a normal-state operator and visual-state action with language-appropriate line comments.
divergence: none
notes: Verified in Lisp and Python buffers.

target_semantics: Repeated SPC v expands the selection through useful syntactic units.
divergence: Expansion is delimiter and line based rather than parser-backed expreg semantics.
notes: The tested word-to-enclosing-pair progression is preserved.

target_semantics: Enable delimiter-safe Lispyville-style operators, atom movement, slurp/barf, and configured structural transforms in all configured Lisp-family buffers.
divergence: none
notes: Exact is limited to the configured key theme and the behavior matrix exercised by the 20 TUI checks.

target_semantics: Jump to visible lines, characters, and symbols with Avy-style target selection.
divergence: Lem uses goto-line, snipe, and symbol isearch rather than on-screen target hints.
notes: Bindings preserve destinations but not Avy interaction semantics.

target_semantics: Match the active Emacs cursor colors for normal, insert, and buffer-local Emacs states, with portable shape cues for visual and replace states.
divergence: Lem renders state color on the cursor cell rather than changing a terminal-wide hardware cursor color; the workwin-only 2px bars and hollow visual cursor are unavailable, so visual remains a default-color box and replace a default-color underline.
notes: NORMAL is a red box, INSERT a green bar, and C-z EMACS a cyan box; C-z returns to the prior buffer-local state, while the frame-multiplexer prefix remains available on C-x t. The real TUI gate checks raw DECSCUSR shapes, rendered SGR color, mark semantics, buffer isolation, prompt restoration, reload, and exit restoration.

target_semantics: Retain Vim linewise operators, counts, dot-repeat, and register semantics alongside custom operator dispatch.
divergence: none
notes: Scope is the explicitly tested operator and register behavior.

target_semantics: Use spaces for indentation with tab width four and allow mode-specific indentation rules.
divergence: none
notes: The focused gate proves the global no-tabs/tab-width defaults and official-CLI-resolved indent_style, indent_size, and tab_width hierarchy in a programming buffer.

target_semantics: Trim trailing whitespace only on lines touched during editing when saving a programming buffer.
divergence: none
notes: The hermetic editing test covers touched-line epochs and exclusions; the formatting gate proves that EditorConfig false/absent retains this policy, while true deliberately adds whole-buffer normalization.

target_semantics: Automatically insert balanced delimiter pairs globally.
divergence: Pair insertion, counts, escapes, local balance reuse, whitespace skipping, prompts, and Paredit integration work; full forward balance scanning across forms, global adjacent-pair Backspace, and zero-result prompt recovery remain absent.
notes: The real TUI exercises Fundamental, Python, Lisp/Paredit, completion, numeric prefixes, odd/even escapes, balanced and unmatched adjacent delimiters, whitespace/newline skipping, read-only buffers, undo, Vi replace/visual isolation, and mode hook preservation.

target_semantics: Typing replaces an active selection.
divergence: none
notes: The exact claim is scoped to the target: ordinary insertion replaces forward or reverse nonempty Emacs-style regions. Openers wrap instead; the TUI verifies mark state, orientation, zero-width marks, one-step undo, read-only buffers, Paredit precedence, and Vi visual isolation. Lem deliberately escapes an unmatched embedded quote to keep a wrapped Lisp string valid rather than reproduce Lispy's raw interior quote.

target_semantics: Provide built-in undo and redo with raised history limits plus a navigable visual undo tree.
divergence: The configured numeric budgets apply to retained UTF-8 edit payload rather than Emacs heap usage; Lem also imposes node, edit, and 128 MiB route-work caps, stores no historical point per node, lacks Vundo numeric prefixes and debug keys i/D, and has no API for discarding speculative transactions.
notes: The real ncurses gate covers retained branches, preferred ordinary undo/redo, branch/stem/save-event navigation, live preview, distant point and view restoration, mark/diff, save and dirty after-save descendants, rollback and accept, delayed-leader bottom-pane restoration, direct and hook-driven teardown, near-cap wide-tree pruning, asymmetric-route and stale-generation refusal, read-only behavior, and private temporary files.

target_semantics: M-j duplicates the active region when present, otherwise the current line.
divergence: Visual-block or rectangle duplication is explicitly unsupported.
notes: The TUI suite covers Emacs-compatible unterminated-EOF newlines, exact point retention, one-step undo, forward and reverse VISUAL selections, V-LINE retention, and Paredit's structural M-j precedence.

target_semantics: Fill the current paragraph and optionally auto-fill while typing at the configured column.
divergence: none
notes: Exact is limited to the tested plain-text paragraph and buffer-local toggle semantics.

target_semantics: Disable lockfiles, backup files, and auto-save files.
divergence: none
notes: The real ncurses gate edits and waits without saving, proves the visited file is unchanged, verifies auto-save remains inactive, and rejects Lem- and Emacs-style backup, lock, and auto-save sidecars.

target_semantics: Toggle visual line wrapping without changing the underlying buffer.
divergence: Emacs visual-line-mode wraps at word boundaries, while Lem wraps at the display-width boundary, so displayed rows can break at different positions.
notes: The UI gate verifies truncated startup and reversible buffer-local wrapping; the 40-column gate verifies that the toggle also reversibly activates the configured Vi policy.

target_semantics: Expose interactive multiple-cursor editing only if the Emacs configuration actually does so.
divergence: Emacs only uses multiple-cursors internally for a streaming cursor overlay.
notes: No interactive port target is implied by package declaration alone.

target_semantics: While visual-line mode is active, give Evil's j/k, gj/gk, 0/g0, $/g$, I/A, D/C, doubled line operators/registers, paste, and V the configured screen-line versus logical-line semantics.
divergence: Emacs visual-line-mode wraps at word boundaries, while Lem wraps at display width, so displayed-row boundaries can differ even though the modal policy matches.
notes: The 40-column ncurses gate covers reversible policy, conditional and inverse motions, counts and goals, endpoints, I/A, characterwise and linewise operators/registers, Visual rows, BOL promotion, paste, undo/redo, CJK/tab widths, empty ranges, boundary clamping, and Lispyville delimiter safety plus its character-register quirk.

target_semantics: Show command candidates automatically; support Prescient literal, regexp, and initialism matching; cycle and persist learned ranking without closing on Space.
divergence: none
notes: The exact claim is scoped to the prompt behaviors exercised by the real TUI suite.

target_semantics: Apply Prescient filtering and learned ranking to buffer-switching prompts while retaining filename annotations.
divergence: none
notes: The real TUI distinguishes same-basename buffers by path, selects one, and verifies that learned ranking moves it first on reopen.

target_semantics: Keep file completion on partial path-component matching while applying learned candidate ranking.
divergence: none
notes: The real TUI verifies automatic refresh, slash-preserving directory acceptance, nested hyphen-component matching, selection, and learned ranking without losing the directory prefix.

target_semantics: Annotate completion candidates with useful contextual metadata.
divergence: M-x preserves keybinding annotations, but broader Marginalia category annotations are absent.
notes: none

target_semantics: Offer automatic in-buffer completion after a 3-character prefix and 0.2-second idle delay in terminal buffers.
divergence: none
notes: The real TUI verifies the threshold, wall-clock delay, debounce, ten-row popup, non-cycling boundaries, singleton display, full-token replacement, movement and buffer-switch cleanup, Escape cancellation, and stale asynchronous rejection.

target_semantics: Compose Cape file and dynamic-abbreviation fallbacks with mode-local completion providers.
divergence: none
notes: Mode-local completion is authoritative, matching live Eglot exclusivity; otherwise same-major-mode dabbrev precedes file-at-point completion, and both fallbacks are exercised through ncurses.

target_semantics: Expand private and community snippets globally with placeholder and field navigation through Tab.
divergence: The audited data-only grammar is implemented, but 144 executable or conditional definitions remain unavailable; stacked sessions, direct bindings, and redo revival are absent.
notes: The real TUI covers fields, defaults, mirrors, nesting, repeated placeholders, zero fields, navigation, completion and Vi precedence, Paredit, indentation, lifecycle cleanup, undo, and a pinned Python community snippet.

target_semantics: Find project files, grep a project, switch projects, and switch among project buffers.
divergence: Consult live preview and identical candidate metadata remain absent; non-Git marker projects retain Lem's broader root semantics, and the shared upstream history file is not transaction-safe across concurrent processes.
notes: The real ncurses gate covers clean-profile auto-registration, fresh-process MRU persistence, tracked and untracked Git files, ignored and sibling exclusion, Emacs regexp conversion, exact file/non-file buffer membership, request-local cancellation, malformed and cyclic submodules, asynchronous bounded search, every configured leader workflow, arbitrary-directory selection, and project dispatch.

target_semantics: Search LSP symbols across the workspace from SPC p s.
divergence: The command sends one bounded workspace/symbol query and then narrows locally with annotated Prescient candidates; Consult's incremental server queries and preview-on-move are absent.
notes: The real ncurses gate verifies project-correct routing, JSON-RPC error recovery, name/kind/container/file annotations, selection, and the final file/line/character jump.

target_semantics: Offer context-sensitive actions for the current candidate or object and integrate those actions with search results.
divergence: Ncurses cannot distinguish C-. so completion-local actions use C-c a; target cycling, act-all, collect/export/live views, arbitrary Embark action-map composition, and richer embark-consult adapters remain unavailable.
notes: The real ncurses suite verifies exact normal/visual leader and completion-local bindings, target-specific dispatch and cancellation, forward/reverse region priority, URL copy, relative and property-backed files, identifier definition/reference delegation, native-menu delegation, completion copy-without-accept and exact-once acceptance, peek locations, stale-target safety, and reload idempotence; LSP code-action gating, external opening, and buffer actions remain source-inspected.

target_semantics: Edit grep result matches in place and write changes back to source files.
divergence: unassessed
notes: Capability survey reports editable grep, but this repository has no behavioral verification.

target_semantics: Keep plain LSP completion display, filtering, and insertion-text precedence distinct; run final acceptance once and reject out-of-order asynchronous results.
divergence: none
notes: The exact claim covers label, filterText, insertText, TextEdit and InsertReplaceEdit newText precedence, tracked full replacement ranges, distinct final-insert and post-accept callbacks, singleton acceptance, per-generation origin validation, and logical cancellation; resolved documentation/detail, item defaults, insertTextMode, and completion commands remain separate gaps.

target_semantics: Apply case-smart, space-separated Orderless matching to in-buffer candidates and expose the Corfu separator workflow.
divergence: CL-PPCRE is not Emacs regexp syntax; the pinned default % character-fold and & annotation dispatchers are absent; initialism is scoped to deterministic ASCII word boundaries.
notes: The real TUI verifies the portable configured matcher subset, raw filtering before the display cap, filterText identity, M-Space local ownership without spaced provider queries, stale-result rejection, zero-match recovery, tracked replacement, manual completion, and prompt/file isolation.

target_semantics: M-s f prompts for a directory and wildcard, finds matching names recursively, and keeps navigable results available after visiting a match.
divergence: The persistent path list lacks Dired marking, file operations, long-list columns, and C-c C-k process cancellation.
notes: The real TUI verifies both prompts, asynchronous results, files and directories, literal Unix metacharacters, control-character escaping, argv safety, buffer ownership, read-only persistence, Vi Return, and q.

target_semantics: Expand the configured private Org jjs snippet, replace its language default, and finish at the blank source-block body position.
divergence: none
notes: The fixture uses the exact private file body and checks text, active field, one-based point, and final session teardown through ncurses.

target_semantics: Run community snippet conditions, command snippets, backquoted Emacs Lisp, and field transforms with Yasnippet semantics.
divergence: The 144 executable or conditional pinned definitions are classified as unavailable and their embedded code is never evaluated.
notes: Data-only expansion is intentionally fail-closed; adding an evaluator without an explicit security model would violate the implementation boundary.

target_semantics: Interpret LSP completion items whose insertTextFormat is Snippet and preserve their placeholder session after acceptance.
divergence: Common Eglot-to-Yasnippet fields, defaults, mirrors, nesting, exits, ranges, and key precedence work, but strict TextMate variables, choices, transforms, and escaping are not implemented.
notes: The real ncurses gate covers pre-rendered fail-closed installation, dynamic capability advertising, format-1 literals, insertText, TextEdit, InsertReplaceEdit.replace, exact-once insertion, mirrors, Tab and Return, malformed recovery, no raw partial syntax, and inert server-supplied backquotes.

target_semantics: Resolve an accepted LSP completion item lazily and apply direct or resolved additionalTextEdits without corrupting its primary insertion.
divergence: Acceptance is synchronously bounded at two seconds, only additionalTextEdits is advertised and imported, invalid or overlapping batches are skipped rather than reproduced with Eglot's order-dependent behavior, and Lem has no native change group for rollback after an arbitrary throwing mutation hook.
notes: The real ncurses gate verifies deferred exact-once resolve, timeout-state cleanup, original-primary preservation, direct and resolved edits around a length-changing snippet, literal $1 additional text, UTF-16 composite ranges, read-only preflight, one-step undo, and fail-closed invalid or overlapping batches.

target_semantics: Start an LSP client for supported local programming buffers while avoiding inappropriate transient or remote buffers.
divergence: Temporary and fileless buffers are excluded and unsupported modes fail cleanly; Lem has no TRAMP-equivalent remote-file transport, while the diagnostics fallback policy is tracked separately.
notes: The hermetic project fixture starts a real stdio server only for file-backed registered modes and verifies the fileless guard; the installed-wrapper gate also proves configuration and LSP attachment occur before a command-line file's caller eval.

target_semantics: Automatically start rust-analyzer for local Rust buffers in the current Emacs project.
divergence: Lem roots each workspace at the nearest Cargo.toml, while Eglot uses the project.el project root, normally the VC root.
notes: The installed-wrapper gate resolves the packaged server and Rust toolchain, completes a real initialize handshake from an automatically selected Rust buffer, asserts its Cargo root and attachment, invokes project shutdown, and proves the client process exits.

target_semantics: When Eglot is started for a Python project, select pyright-langserver over stdio.
divergence: Lem starts Pyright automatically and roots at the nearest Python metadata marker; the current Emacs config has no Python Eglot hook and uses the project.el root after manual startup.
notes: The installed-wrapper gate resolves packaged pyright-langserver, completes a real stdio handshake from an automatically selected Python buffer, asserts workspace ownership, and proves clean project shutdown.

target_semantics: Automatically start harper-ls over stdio for local Markdown prose buffers.
divergence: Lem has one Markdown mode and roots Harper at the nearest .git directory; Emacs hooks markdown-mode and markdown-ts-mode separately and uses their project.el root.
notes: The installed-wrapper gate proves a real pipe-backed Harper handshake, observes Harper's workspace/configuration request, verifies post-initialize liveness, attachment, root, capabilities, and process cleanup; the project LSP gate separately proves diagnostic publication and overlay cleanup without depending on Harper's version-specific prose rules.

target_semantics: Start nixd for local Nix buffers, derive nixpkgs and known option expressions from the nearest flake, and configure formatting when a formatter is executable.
divergence: Emacs supplies its outer :nixd section through workspace configuration and its declared daemon PATH normally contains no formatter candidate; Lem sends the equivalent inner map as initializationOptions, packages nixfmt-rfc-style, and chooses the nearest Nix marker rather than the project.el root.
notes: The installed-wrapper gate completes a real nixd handshake and checks executable provenance, canonical ownership, the frozen initialization-options map, formatter availability, and clean process shutdown.

target_semantics: Automatically start gopls for local Go buffers in the current Emacs project.
divergence: Emacs runs gopls over boosted stdio at the project.el root; upstream Lem runs gopls serve -port over TCP at the nearest go.mod and additionally sets completeUnimported and matcher initialization options.
notes: The installed-wrapper gate resolves packaged gopls, completes the real TCP handshake, checks mode, spec, root, attachment, capabilities, and initialization options, then terminates both client and server process.

target_semantics: Use Eclipse JDT language services and Google Java formatting for Java buffers.
divergence: No jdtls spec or launcher integration is configured.

notes: The TUI gate covers CLI-first manual formatting, stable point/mark and undo, final save rewrite ordering, direct argv plus timeout, CLI-process failure isolation, prose exclusion, and reload safety.

target_semantics: Use LSP diagnostics when managed and a non-LSP linter otherwise.

target_semantics: For project-scoped local-file language servers, own one server per canonical root, deduplicate and bound startup, migrate buffer ownership, and restart or stop one project without disturbing another.

notes: The real stdio/ncurses gate covers concurrent same-root startup, cross-root isolation and save-as migration, mode/diagnostic/handler cleanup, initialization timeout, idle retention/reuse/explicit stop, project-only restart, bounded shutdown with forced disposal, graceful exit on responsive paths, PID death, and editor-exit cleanup; a runtime contract separately preserves Lem Lisp-v2's deliberately global connection selection.

target_semantics: Automatically start terraform-ls for local Terraform buffers in the current Emacs project.

target_semantics: Honor EditorConfig rules in programming buffers.
divergence: Only the documented core property mapping is applied, and charset changes occur after initial decoding and affect subsequent writes only; arbitrary EditorConfig plugin behavior is not reproduced.
notes: The official CLI supplies hierarchy, root, and unset semantics for every steady-state local file buffer; the TUI covers indentation, LF/CR/CRLF, subsequent-write Latin-1, trailing whitespace, and final-newline behavior, while max_line_length mapping remains source-verified.

target_semantics: Start quietly and quickly without toolbars, menus, startup text, or configuration compilation noise.

target_semantics: Start a reusable editor server and point EDITOR, VISUAL, and GIT_EDITOR at its client.
divergence: The configured wrapper starts a standalone ncurses editor process, not an Emacsclient-equivalent daemon workflow.

target_semantics: Use ripgrep for project search with useful defaults and project-aware ignore behavior.
divergence: none
notes: SPC p g converts Emacs regexp syntax, passes it after --, searches the exact tracked-plus-untracked project file set in bounded argv batches, and renders editable peek results off the editor thread.

target_semantics: Automatically revert changed files and persist places, prompt history, the kill ring, and searches.
divergence: Command-triggered polling replaces Emacs idle timers and filesystem notifications; same-metadata rewrites above 16 MiB are detected on explicit save but not periodic scans; transactional reload snapshots are capped at 64 MiB; saved places cover local files but not directory buffers; non-file buffers require explicit adapters; prompt persistence is a reviewed default-deny subset; oversized entries remain live but are omitted; stale processes can resurrect cleared histories.
notes: The 45-check ncurses gate covers safe clean and dirty external changes, large/deleted/new-file stale-save guards, Save As races, modified quit refusal, fresh-process file positions, separate literal/regexp history, prompt privacy/live caps, physical Vi-aware kill order, bounded non-evaluating state reads, private modes, failure-safe exit, and stale concurrent-process merging. Recent files remain split out under MISC-013.

target_semantics: Compile configuration ahead of time and avoid writing compiled artifacts into the source tree.
divergence: The Nix wrapper redirects ASDF outputs to cache and loads on demand rather than AOT native-compiling every source.
notes: none

target_semantics: Persist a deduplicated MRU of at most 300 recent files across editor processes and open it with M-g r.
divergence: none
notes: A pre-seeded oversized history is trimmed newest-first, index-reset, rewritten, and reloaded; reopening an older file then moves one entry to the front, and Return opens it.

target_semantics: Automatically persist modified bookmarks at exit and restore them in a fresh editor process.
divergence: Pinned Lem provides explicit bookmark save and load commands but lem-yath does not automatically invoke them or isolate and merge bookmark state.
notes: Bookmark creation and jumping remain available on SPC b m and SPC RET; this row tracks the configured Emacs lifecycle rather than the commands themselves.

target_semantics: Treat language tooling, VCS, notes, debugging, and AI entry points as the second acceptance tier.

target_semantics: Track bespoke applications and host-gated integrations explicitly even where Lem has no counterpart.
